term_inventory_hebrew.meaning: Afraid, fearing, God-fearing; adjective/participle form of the ya.re root; describes a person who fears someone or God. 'Those who fear the LORD' is a key OT designation for the devout.
To fear, dread, be afraid; (Qal) to fear, dread. The verb form corresponding to the adjective fearing (ya.gor H3016). Used of personal dread and fearful apprehension.
(Qal) to fear; meaning uncertain; hapax legomenon. Occurs only in Isa 44:8, paired with ya.re in a context of reassurance. The precise semantic distinction from ya.re is unclear.

To fear, revere, be afraid; (Qal) to fear, be afraid; to stand in awe of, be awed; (Niphal) to be fearful, dreadful, be feared; to cause astonishment and awe; to inspire reverence or godly fear; (Piel) to make afraid, terrify. STEP subdivided sense H3372H labelled as the fear/afraid sense. The most frequent fear verb in the OT.

To fear, revere, be afraid; STEP subdivided sense H3372G labelled as the revere/awe sense. Sense label 'frightening(DANGER)' appears in the meaning block. Same root as H3372H; the subdivision reflects different contextual emphases in STEP's tagging. Both H3372G and H3372H share the same 314-occurrence count and largely the same verse pool.

To hasten, make haste, hurry; (Qal) to hasten; (Niphal) to be hurried, be anxious; (Piel) to hasten, make haste, prepare quickly. The fear-relevant sense is the Niphal: to be anxious/hurried. STEP H4116A is a subdivided sense.

To storm, rage; (Qal) to storm, be stormy; (Niphal) to be enraged; (Piel) to storm away; (Pual) to be driven by storm. Included in the fear word list via the association of storm/rage with dread. Primary meaning is meteorological or emotional rage.

To dissolve, melt; (Qal) to waste away; (Niphal) to melt, vanish, drop off, faint, grow fearful; (Hiphil) to cause to melt. The fear sense arises from the Niphal: to grow fearful, to faint with fear. Used frequently of hearts melting with dread - a vivid somatic metaphor for paralysing fear.

To see, perceive, behold; specifically the seer/vision sense of ra.ah in STEP's subdivision. Total occurrences of root H7200: approximately 1298. Fear-relevant verses: 8 only, as noted explicitly in source file. STEP H7200B is a subdivided sense.

To tremble, quake, be afraid, be startled, be terrified; (Qal) to tremble of mountains or people, to be anxiously careful, to go trembling; (Hiphil) to cause to tremble, to drive in terror, rout. A strongly somatic term - the physical act of trembling induced by fear.

To tremble, dread, fear, oppress, prevail, break, be terrified; (Qal) to cause to tremble, terrify; (Niphal) to be awesome, terrible; (Hiphil) to regard with awe, terrify. Covers both the experience of terror and the causing of it.

Fear, anxiety, quaking, trembling; extreme anxiety, anxious care; (a) trembling, quaking; (b) anxious care. The noun form of cha.rad - the experience of trembling fear. Used of the terror of divine encounter, the anguish of alarm, and the trembling that accompanies crisis.

Trembling, fearful, afraid; adjective form of the cha.rad root. Used notably of 'those who tremble at the word of God' - a reverential fear that shapes obedience (Ezr 9:4; 10:3; Isa 66:2, 5). Describes a posture of attentive, fearful reverence before God's word.

Trembling; the experience of trembling with fear; STEP H7461B is a subdivided sense, with H7461A (ra.ad - trembling) as a related form. Used in contexts of extreme fear, divine awe, and existential dread.

verse_records.verse_text: He said, "Do not lay your hand on the boy or do anything to him, for now I know that you fear God, seeing you have not withheld your son, your only son, from me."
But the midwives feared God and did not do as the king of Egypt commanded them, but let the male children live.
And because the midwives feared God, he gave them families.
Then whoever feared the word of the LORD among the servants of Pharaoh hurried his slaves and his livestock into the houses,

Moreover, look for able men from all the people, men who fear God, who are trustworthy and hate a bribe, and place such men over the people as chiefs of thousands, of hundreds, of fifties, and of tens.
You shall not curse the deaf or put a stumbling block before the blind, but you shall fear your God: I am the LORD.
"You shall stand up before the gray head and honor the face of an old man, and you shall fear your God: I am the LORD.
You shall not wrong one another, but you shall fear your God, for I am the LORD your God.
Take no interest from him or profit, but fear your God, that your brother may live beside you.
You shall not rule over him ruthlessly but shall fear your God.

"And now, Israel, what does the LORD your God require of you, but to fear the LORD your God, to walk in all his ways, to love him, to serve the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul,
how he attacked you on the way when you were faint and weary, and cut off your tail, those who were lagging behind you, and he did not fear God.
I gave my brother Hanani and Hananiah the governor of the castle charge over Jerusalem, for he was a more faithful and God-fearing man than many.
There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job, and that man was blameless and upright, one who feared God and turned away from evil.
And the LORD said to Satan, "Have you considered my servant Job, that there is none like him on the earth, a blameless and upright man, who fears God and turns away from evil?"

And the LORD said to Satan, "Have you considered my servant Job, that there is none like him on the earth, a blameless and upright man, who fears God and turns away from evil? He still holds fast his integrity, although you incited me against him to destroy him without reason."
in whose eyes a vile person is despised, but who honors those who fear the LORD; who swears to his own hurt and does not change;
You who fear the LORD, praise him! All you offspring of Jacob, glorify him, and stand in awe of him, all you offspring of Israel!
Who is the man who fears the LORD? Him will he instruct in the way that he should choose.
The friendship of the LORD is for those who fear him, and he makes known to them his covenant.
Oh, how abundant is your goodness, which you have stored up for those who fear you and worked for those who take refuge in you, in the sight of the children of mankind!
Behold, the eye of the LORD is on those who fear him, on those who hope in his steadfast love,
The angel of the LORD encamps around those who fear him, and delivers them.
Oh, fear the LORD, you his saints, for those who fear him have no lack!
You have set up a banner for those who fear you, that they may flee to it from the bow. Selah
Surely his salvation is near to those who fear him, that glory may dwell in our land.
For as high as the heavens are above the earth, so great is his steadfast love toward those who fear him;
As a father shows compassion to his children, so the LORD shows compassion to those who fear him.
But the steadfast love of the LORD is from everlasting to everlasting on those who fear him, and his righteousness to children's children,
He provides food for those who fear him; he remembers his covenant forever.
Praise the LORD! Blessed is the man who fears the LORD, who greatly delights in his commandments!
You who fear the LORD, trust in the LORD! He is their help and their shield.
he will bless those who fear the LORD, both the small and the great.
Let those who fear the LORD say, "His steadfast love endures forever."
Blessed is everyone who fears the LORD, who walks in his ways!
Behold, thus shall the man be blessed who fears the LORD.
Charm is deceitful, and beauty is vain, but a woman who fears the LORD is to be praised.
For I was afraid of the anger and hot displeasure that the LORD bore against you, so that he was ready to destroy you. But the LORD listened to me that time also.
And he will bring upon you again all the diseases of Egypt, of which you were afraid, and they shall cling to you.
For the thing that I fear comes upon me, and what I dread befalls me.
I become afraid of all my suffering, for I know you will not hold me innocent.
Turn away the reproach that I dread, for your rules are good.
Fear not, nor be afraid; have I not told you from of old and declared it? And you are my witnesses! Is there a God besides me? There is no Rock; I know not any."
And he said, "I heard the sound of you in the garden, and I was afraid, because I was naked, and I hid myself."
After these things the word of the LORD came to Abram in a vision: "Fear not, Abram, I am your shield; your reward shall be very great."
But Sarah denied it, saying, "I did not laugh," for she was afraid. He said, "No, but you did laugh."
Now Lot went up out of Zoar and lived in the hills with his two daughters, for he was afraid to live in Zoar. So he lived in a cave with his two daughters.
So Abimelech rose early in the morning and called all his servants and told them all these things. And the men were very much afraid.
And God heard the voice of the boy, and the angel of God called to Hagar from heaven and said to her, "What troubles you, Hagar? Fear not, for God has heard the voice of the boy where he is.
When the men of the place asked him about his wife, he said, "She is my sister," for he feared to say, "My wife," thinking, "lest the men of the place should kill me because of Rebekah."
And the LORD appeared to him the same night and said, "I am the God of Abraham your father. Fear not, for I am with you and will bless you and multiply your offspring for my servant Abraham's sake."
And he was afraid and said, "How awesome is this place! This is none other than the house of God, and this is the gate of heaven."
Jacob answered and said to Laban, "Because I was afraid, for I thought that you would take your daughters from me by force.
Then Jacob was greatly afraid and distressed. He divided the people who were with him, and the flocks and herds and camels, into two camps,
And when her labor was at its hardest, the midwife said to her, "Do not fear, for you have another son."
As they emptied their sacks, behold, every man's bundle of money was in his sack. And when they and their father saw their bundles of money, they were afraid.

And the men were afraid because they were brought to Joseph's house, and they said, "It is because of the money, which was replaced in our sacks the first time, that we are brought in, so that he may assault us and fall upon us to make us servants and seize our donkeys."
He replied, "Peace to you, do not be afraid. Your God and the God of your father has put treasure in your sacks for you. I received your money." Then he brought Simeon out to them.
Then he said, "I am God, the God of your father. Do not be afraid to go down to Egypt, for there I will make you into a great nation.
But Joseph said to them, "Do not fear, for am I in the place of God?
So do not fear; I will provide for you and your little ones." Thus he comforted them and spoke kindly to them.
But the midwives feared God and did not do as the king of Egypt commanded them, but let the male children live.
And because the midwives feared God, he gave them families.
He answered, "Who made you a prince and a judge over us? Do you mean to kill me as you killed the Egyptian?" Then Moses was afraid, and thought, "Surely the thing is known."
And he said, "I am the God of your father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob." And Moses hid his face, for he was afraid to look at God.
But as for you and your servants, I know that you do not yet fear the LORD God."
When Pharaoh drew near, the people of Israel lifted up their eyes, and behold, the Egyptians were marching after them, and they feared greatly. And the people of Israel cried out to the LORD.
And Moses said to the people, "Fear not, stand firm, and see the salvation of the LORD, which he will work for you today. For the Egyptians whom you see today, you shall never see again.
Israel saw the great power that the LORD used against the Egyptians, so the people feared the LORD, and they believed in the LORD and in his servant Moses.
Moses said to the people, "Do not fear, for God has come to test you, that the fear of him may be before you, that you may not sin."
Aaron and all the people of Israel saw Moses, and behold, the skin of his face shone, and they were afraid to come near him.
See, the LORD your God has set the land before you. Go up, take possession, as the LORD, the God of your fathers, has told you. Do not fear or be dismayed.'

that you may fear the LORD your God, you and your son and your son's son, by keeping all his statutes and his commandments, which I command you, all the days of your life, and that your days may be long.
It is the LORD your God you shall fear. Him you shall serve and by his name you shall swear.

You shall fear the LORD your God. You shall serve him and hold fast to him, and by his name you shall swear.
You shall walk after the LORD your God and fear him and keep his commandments and obey his voice, and you shall serve him and hold fast to him.
And all the people shall hear and fear and not act presumptuously again.

"When you go out to war against your enemies, and see horses and chariots and an army larger than your own, you shall not be afraid of them, for the LORD your God is with you, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt.

and some Hebrews crossed the fords of the Jordan to the land of Gad and Gilead. Saul was still at Gilgal, and all the people followed him trembling.
And there was a panic in the camp, in the field, and among all the people. The garrison and even the raiders trembled, the earth quaked, and it became a very great panic.
Samuel did what the LORD commanded and came to Bethlehem. The elders of the city came to meet him trembling and said, "Do you come peaceably?"
Then David came to Nob, to Ahimelech the priest. And Ahimelech came to meet David, trembling and said to him, "Why are you alone, and no one with you?"
When Saul saw the army of the Philistines, he was afraid, and his heart trembled greatly.
I will come upon him while he is weary and discouraged and throw him into a panic, and all the people who are with him will flee. I will strike down only the king,
Then all the guests of Adonijah trembled and rose, and each went his own way.
"At this also my heart trembles and leaps out of its place.
they have crossed over the pass; at Geba they lodge for the night; Ramah trembles; Gibeah of Saul has fled.
In that day the Egyptians will be like women, and tremble with fear before the hand that the LORD of hosts shakes over them.

"Then fear not, O Jacob my servant, declares the LORD, nor be dismayed, O Israel; for behold, I will save you from far away, and your offspring from the land of their captivity. Jacob shall return and have quiet and ease, and none shall make him afraid.

Then all the princes of the sea will step down from their thrones and remove their robes and strip off their embroidered garments. They will clothe themselves with trembling; they will sit on the ground and tremble every moment and be appalled at you.
Now the coastlands tremble on the day of your fall, and the coastlands that are on the sea are dismayed at your passing.'
They shall go after the LORD; he will roar like a lion; when he roars, his children shall come trembling from the west;
they shall come trembling like birds from Egypt, and like doves from the land of Assyria, and I will return them to their homes, declares the LORD.

And I said, "What are these coming to do?" He said, "These are the horns that scattered Judah, so that no one raised his head. And these have come to terrify them, to cast down the horns of the nations who lifted up their horns against the land of Judah to scatter it."
Then I said to you, 'Do not be in dread or afraid of them.
You shall not be in dread of them, for the LORD your God is in your midst, a great and awesome God.
and shall say to them, 'Hear, O Israel, today you are drawing near for battle against your enemies: let not your heart faint. Do not fear or panic or be in dread of them,
Be strong and courageous. Do not fear or be in dread of them, for it is the LORD your God who goes with you. He will not leave you or forsake you."
Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be frightened, and do not be dismayed, for the LORD your God is with you wherever you go."
Will you frighten a driven leaf and pursue dry chaff?
because I stood in great fear of the multitude, and the contempt of families terrified me, so that I kept silence, and did not go out of doors-
to do justice to the fatherless and the oppressed, so that man who is of the earth may strike terror no more.
a God greatly to be feared in the council of the holy ones, and awesome above all who are around him?
And people shall enter the caves of the rocks and the holes of the ground, from before the terror of the LORD, and from the splendor of his majesty, when he rises to terrify the earth.
to enter the caverns of the rocks and the clefts of the cliffs, from before the terror of the LORD, and from the splendor of his majesty, when he rises to terrify the earth.
"Do not call conspiracy all that this people calls conspiracy, and do not fear what they fear, nor be in dread.
But the LORD of hosts, him you shall honor as holy. Let him be your fear, and let him be your dread.
For when he sees his children, the work of my hands, in his midst, they will sanctify my name; they will sanctify the Holy One of Jacob and will stand in awe of the God of Israel.

All these things my hand has made, and so all these things came to be, declares the LORD. But this is the one to whom I will look: he who is humble and contrite in spirit and trembles at my word.

Hear the word of the LORD, you who tremble at his word: "Your brothers who hate you and cast you out for my name's sake have said, 'Let the LORD be glorified, that we may see your joy'; but it is they who shall be put to shame.

dread came upon me, and trembling, which made all my bones shake.

data_quality_flags.description: to fear (ya.re H3372H): ~314 occurrences stated; approximately 65 verses extracted. Well below the 20% threshold. Source file note confirms partial verse set.
to fear: revere (ya.re H3372G): ~314 occurrences stated; approximately 46 verses extracted. Well below the 20% threshold. Verse overlap with H3372H expected.
afraid (ya.re H3373): ~65 occurrences stated; approximately 37 verses extracted. Below the 20% threshold.

to hasten (ma.har H4116A): ~68 occurrences stated; approximately 20 verses extracted. Primary meaning is haste, not fear. Fear link via Niphal (to be anxious) only. Phase 2 to assess domain relevance.

to fear (ya.re): MTI provisional numbers H3372A, H3372B, H3372C corrected. Source file uses H3372H (fear/afraid sense) and H3372G (revere/awe sense) as two subdivided entries. H3372C appears to have no source-file equivalent and should be removed from MTI.

to see: seer (ra.ah H7200B): root H7200 has ~1298 total occurrences; only 8 fear-relevant verses extracted as noted explicitly in source file. Occurrence count recorded as 8 (fear-relevant only).

to rage (sa.ar H5590): primary meaning is storm/rage, not fear. Phase 2 to assess domain relevance. Source file STEP term list heading mistakenly reads 'to know (sa.ar)' - corrected to 'to rage' per word analysis block content.

to fear: revere (ya.re H3372G) and to fear (ya.re H3372H): both carry occurrence count ~314 - this is the count for the whole ya.re root. The STEP subdivision into H3372G and H3372H does not split the count. Verse overlap between the two entries is expected and should be noted in Session B.
